introSection: Tokyo Dome City Attractions (東京ドームシティアトラクションズ) is an amusement park located next to the Tokyo Dome in Bunkyō, Tokyo, Japan, and forms a part of the Tokyo Dome City entertainment complex. It opened in 1958, and was formerly known as Korakuen Amusement Park (後楽園ゆうえんち) until April 2003. It was one of the most popular amusement parks in Tokyo. Rides include the Big O Ferris wheel and Thunder Dolphin roller coaster.

sections.title: LaQua
sections.content:
* Big-O (Ferris wheel)
 * Thunder Dolphin (roller coaster)
 * Wonder Drop (water slide)
 * Venus Lagoon (merry-go-round)
 * The Dive (shooting ride)
 * Water Symphony (fountain show)

sections.title: Viking Zone
sections.content:
* Bun Bun Bee
 * Carousel
 * Ba-Ba-Ba Viking (swinging ship)

sections.content:
* Pixie Cup (teacups)
 * Power Tower
 * Corocco
 * Magical Mist

sections.content:
* Bloom Express

* Panic Coaster – Back Daaan

sections.title: Viking Zone
sections.content:
* Super Viking SORABUNE (swinging ship)
 * Furi Furi Grand Prix (go-cart)
 * Tower Hacker (drop tower)
 * Kids Hacker (mini drop tower)

sections.title: Tower Zone
sections.content:
The area was formerly known as Parachute Zone until August 2024.
 * Chapu Chapu Creek
 * Linear Gale (roller coaster)
 * Sky Flower
 * Spinning Coaster Maihime (roller coaster)

sections.title: Geopolis
sections.content:
* Tokyo Panic Cruise
 * Lupin III: Labyrinth Trap
 * Geopanic

sections.title: Accidents
sections.content:
* On 29 February 2010, a 26-year-old worker lost three fingers while inspecting the Tower Hacker ride before the park opened in the morning.
 * On 5 December 2010, a nine-year-old girl suffered minor injuries after being hit by a bolt which had fallen from the Thunder Dolphin roller coaster.
 * On 30 January 2011, a 34-year-old man was killed after falling from the Spinning Coaster Maihime roller coaster at the park.

fullText:
Tokyo Dome City Attractions (東京ドームシティアトラクションズ) is an amusement park located next to the Tokyo Dome in Bunkyō, Tokyo, Japan, and forms a part of the Tokyo Dome City entertainment complex. It opened in 1958, and was formerly known as Korakuen Amusement Park (後楽園ゆうえんち) until April 2003. It was one of the most popular amusement parks in Tokyo. Rides include the Big O Ferris wheel and Thunder Dolphin roller coaster.




 * Big-O (Ferris wheel)
 * Thunder Dolphin (roller coaster)
 * Wonder Drop (water slide)
 * Venus Lagoon (merry-go-round)
 * The Dive (shooting ride)
 * Water Symphony (fountain show)


 * Bun Bun Bee

 * Super Viking SORABUNE (swinging ship)
 * Furi Furi Grand Prix (go-cart)
 * Tower Hacker (drop tower)
 * Kids Hacker (mini drop tower)

The area was formerly known as Parachute Zone until August 2024.
 * Chapu Chapu Creek
 * Linear Gale (roller coaster)
 * Sky Flower
 * Spinning Coaster Maihime (roller coaster)


 * Tokyo Panic Cruise
 * Lupin III: Labyrinth Trap
 * Geopanic


 * MagiQuest Mega Kingdom


 * On 29 February 2010, a 26-year-old worker lost three fingers while inspecting the Tower Hacker ride before the park opened in the morning.
 * On 5 December 2010, a nine-year-old girl suffered minor injuries after being hit by a bolt which had fallen from the Thunder Dolphin roller coaster.
 * On 30 January 2011, a 34-year-old man was killed after falling from the Spinning Coaster Maihime roller coaster at the park.
